title: ED safeguarding documentation

presenting_history.text: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.
presenting_history.rationale: For adult women experiencing intimate-partner violence, the exact WHO documentation section supports consent-based recording of concerns, symptoms, signs and injuries; it does not cover all adult safeguarding groups.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook

examination.text: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.
examination.rationale: For children and young people, the exact NICE section supports recording patient concerns, consent-based assessment and completed risk or consent discussion in the child’s actual words where appropriate.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.

referral.text: Supports recognising indirect disclosure, using open non-leading questions, explaining next steps and recording local safeguarding referral when clinician-completed.
referral.rationale: For children and young people, the exact recognition section supports associated context, relevant negatives, clinician risk context, safeguarding concern and clinician-completed action without asserting abuse.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports recognising indirect disclosure, using open non-leading questions, explaining next steps and recording local safeguarding referral when clinician-completed.

referral.text: Supports observing the child and interactions, communicating directly and privately where appropriate, non-leading exploration and structured family or care-environment context.
referral.rationale: The exact child-assessment section supports focused assessment, appearance, clinician plan, specialist discussion and arranged handover only when performed.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports observing the child and interactions, communicating directly and privately where appropriate, non-leading exploration and structured family or care-environment context.

sources_used.title: Child abuse and neglect
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; UAE safeguarding law and pathways govern local use

sources_used.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook
sources_used.jurisdiction: International guidance requiring UAE legal, safeguarding and referral adaptation

source_limitations: Vulnerable adults, men, elder abuse, trafficking, exact disclosure, immediate danger, dependants, capacity, injuries, forensic needs, reporting law, referral and safety planning remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
